channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why is US consumer sentiment so low — 29 replies — 80.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-05-24 23:21 UTC

OP

Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Early replies (first 3)

62269461
>>62269455
>be burger
>vote retard
>retard do trade war and real war
>cost living up wtf

62269462
>>62269455
they have no money?

62269465
>>62269461
basically this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269478
>>62269461
cry more libtard

62269489
everyone is drinking bearish propaganda

62269502
>>62269455
Sales at my company are down 95% from last year

62269508
A fucking snickers bar costs like $3

I used to buy them with pocket change as a kid, everything sucks, shit quality, and is expensive as fuck.

62269526
Damn, who would have thought expanding the money supply by 40% during covid would demolish wagies purchasing power by 40%

62269530
Bro just use a.i. and you're good
Stop being a doomer

62269542
America used to export inflation to other countries and now dollar is falling against every currency in the world. What happened?

62269549
>>62269455
Shut

Up

62269560
>>62269455
They want to do more than just look at porn for some reason

62269610
>>62269455
Half of Americans are finding themselves on the wrong side of the K shaped economy.

>>62269526
The greatest demonstration of why basic income would never work. Give everyone $1000 a month and everything just goes up in price to compensate.

62269614
>>62269461
>vote retard
What else was on the ballot?

62269636
>>62269614
A BLUE RETARD

62269639
it's just that time of month. i feel most business startups are just entertainment for the proles. the businesses that do matter and make an impact on advancing research costs a lot and is bound to recieve government attention. not everyone can be a rocket scientist. maybe a lot can, but only if they had all the resources and time, but as we know we are limited pretending not to. statistics don't lie that we're in a pinch. why not just become slaves again? why not concentrate all the resources? yes there is a lot of potential for the young, but why prioritize them when you have people that already know what they are doing? the young should realize this, for if not knowing how to struggle, the more resources they will drain anyways. i don't mean this to be ragebait btw, but when times are tough people tend to move to extremes and things get scary

62269641
>>62269610
>the greatest demonstration of why basic income would never work. Give everyone $1000 a month and everything just goes up in price to compensate.
the point is to fundamentally change how incentives are doled out. instead of giving massive subsidies to corporations that in turn waste money on AI and jack up prices on everything, the destitute formerly employed wagies are given money to buy food and pay rent.

62269643
Miracle economy

62269647
>>62269461
Jews control every voteable optio

62269718
>>62269455
AI taking all our jerbs

62269725
I went to canes, a notriously overpriced and overrated chicken place today at noon.

The drive through was packed and when I say packed there was 40+ cars in line. There were so many people that there was a line into the parking lot and then out of it into the other parking lot across the restaurant, with the cars having to make a space for traffic to pass.

On top of that they parking lot for canes was full and the restaurant was full.


So if people are so doom and gloom poor, complaining that everything is expensive why was there 70-80 people eating lunch at an overpriced chicken joint at max capacity

62269728
>>62269455
ONLY REAL ANSWER IN THE WHOLE FUCKING THREAD: the methodology changed, the survey is now mostly democrat, instead of being 50/50 democrat/republican. Actually the sentiment of either democrats and republicans has not changed for some time now... however if the % of democrats slowly goes up in the survey then you will see the consumer sentiment value drop over time.

62269747
>>62269461
>>vote retard
RETARDS ARE THE ONLY OPTIONS WE'RE EVER GIVEN

62269798
>>62269461
MIGA
I
G
A

62269874
>>62269455
Because people are cooked.
Something broke after covid, a lot of people stopped living in the real world and became citizens of the internet instead.
The economy is doing okay, maybe even good, but the doom and gloom that is pervasive on the internet, has led a lot of people to believe otherwise.

62269888
>>62269455
Job market. Who wants to make big purchase when it's so hard find a new job.

62269909
>>62269725
It's not that overpriced $14 for 3(4?) tenders and coleslaw, fries n toast plus drink? That's pretty much any fast food place anymore. The big arch meal was $17>
People are depressed and regular food is expensive as hell too. It used to be buying food at the grocery store was so much cheaper you can't justify it, but now it is only so much cheaper and tastes worse if you suck at cooking, many do.
However the amount of "I'm only eating beans" posting I see makes me think that is also happening.

62269918
>>62269874
Wew lawd to be able to cope this hard, may I have a puff of that?

62270130
>>62269455
because that retarded orange nigger decided to double the debt, rape consumer pockets with his tariffs, then gift them to corpofaggots. Fuck that dumbass, braindead, dipshit, rapist, incompetent, pissant little nigger. Fuck you too cunt.

/BIZ/ YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-24 23:29 UTC

62270147

fuck i losted

item.title: How to make money? — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How to make money?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-24 23:41 UTC

OP

>Starting to invest is pointless now without large capital, times when you could turn 10k into 100k+ are long gone and you will have 20% max profit after 5 years.
>Getting a job or starting a business is impossible (especially if you live in EU/rural area).
Most wealth is concentrated within boomers/gen X/older millenials. They are also feminist paypigs that love throwing money at women for no reason.
The best strategy to make money now is to create an AI model and extract money from retarded boomers while pretending to be an e-girl. Opinions?
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t3aA8QstXuQ

Early replies (first 3)

62267121
>>62266678
>10k into 100k+ are long gone

What the fuck are you talking about, just because you are too retarded to find good ventures doesn't mean you should FUD the board, FAGGOT

62267135
>>62266678
in the US most ruralfags run businesses because there aren't any jobs and most corporations can't afford to be there serving the 5 people in a 20 mile radius

it's a running joke. I meet someone in my little rural town I'll ask if they wash windows or shampoo carpets and they'll laugh knowingly and tell me they tint windows and apply roll-on bedliners and then we'll both have a laugh and go get a beer. Ok that's probably all made up, but it's close to the truth. Rural people usually get by running some sort of small business.

selling drugs and breeing dogs are also popular

62270179
>>62266678
Make Mephedrone.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: why does this keep happening — 125 replies — 194.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 125 • Updated: 2026-05-24 23:04 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269065
>>62268992
should i keep going?

62269070
here have another. there's plenty more where that came from

62269088
I honestly hope sergey rugpulls link and runs off with the money, sending it to zero, so I no longer have to read these dumb threads.

It's been 5 years. You didn't sell the top and you're still holding 5 years later.

62269101
>>62268992
wakey wakey, faggot
bumping this thread so yids and troons can call people incels, cucks etc. and to take their meds

62269132
>>62268931
>he area where alts are likely to stall out or not continue is that zone
Holy shit were you lobotomized? Link didn't "stall out", Bitcoin crashed the entire market.

>>62268956
>why would btc need to dump to keep ĺink down
I sure wish it didn't, but it does. All the fucking time.

62269280
>>62268565
>i can almost gurantee you these anons are repeating the same narratives in their respective echo chambers
nobody gives a shit about twitter posts after the dump takes place you stupid faggot, that's not what we're discussing.
show a single example of btc shitting the bed on news of any crypto project. go on.

you can't, thats why you try to muddy the waters.

62269410
>>62269132
>>why would btc need to dump to keep ĺink down when link doesn't need the help and dumps all on its own?
>I sure wish it didn't, but it does. All the fucking time.
then let's hear it, why do you think it happens? are you already at that stage where you can say it out loud or is it still embarrassing?

62269433
>>62269022
based schizoid anon.

I can't comprehend one thing. Why they are doing this? This has no sense. It would be easier to just buy link with low price as it is now rather than grand scheme dumping b t c to foreshadow Lin.

62269448
>>62269433
checked
my pet theory has always been its to keep price low so bigger players can slurp the "dumps" without sending it. as long as there is more link to be dumped, if you want to accumulate as much as possible, you are incentivized to keep the price low/stagnant. the mechanisms that will make the price pump organically are staking and institutional usage, neither of which are here yet (real staking not the poverty airdrop style staking which currently exists), so game theory dictates you keep the price low and continue to slurp the "dumps". this is assuming you're an entity that's already independently wealthy and just trying to dominate the future. the way you do this is by dumping your bitcoin bags to ensure link stays low, allowing you to continue to slurp. the evidence of this would be the fact link whale wallets continue to accumulate, while smaller wallets are shrinking, so whatever they're doing, its working.

62269628
>>62269448
You have my attention. How many LINK do I need to buy to have made it?

62269640
>>62269628
Don't bother buying now. Wait 2 years or so, that will be when Chainlink Labs is more or less about to run out of tokens to dump.

62269657
>>62269448
if anyone had enough money in bitcoin to manipulate the market, they could just go up to chainlink and tell them "we'll buy all your remaining tokens, $5 each". or are you saying chainlink wouldn't accept this deal? come on

62269661
>>62269657
So Sergey would buy all his remaining tokens? Makes sense.

62269680
>>62269640
How much do you think 1 LINK will be worth in 3 years, when they have all come out?

62269681
>there are people who are not paid fudders who rush into link threads to be the first to post anime children, they are constantly monitoring /biz/

62269695
>>62269681
Troon goon paid fudderoons

62269701
>>62269680
>How much do you think 1 LINK will be worth in 3 years, when they have all come out?
About $5-8. Then we violently pump for a decade or so.

62269710
>>62269661
are you saying sergey is manipulating the market to keep the link price down?

62269723
>>62269710
Yes, this is how he distributes the token to his banking buddies for cheap while keeping up the level playing field theater, makes the project look more organic, chic, crypto-native.

62269753
>>62269628
nobody knows
on the one hand if the market as is stays relatively stable, link will at worst hit #2 marketcap meaning 1k sui/10k makeit was correct and likely even less than that
on the other, its feasible there could be a larger dotcom crash still to come for crypto, a great reset if you will, where link emerges from the wreckage at #2 or #1, but with everything valued much lower at that point.
only thing that seems certain is if crypto is here to stay, link is inevitable.

62269759
>>62269723
if all the big players are in on it, then who are they keeping up the charade for?

62269780
>>62269759
The unwashed masses who will ask
>Chain-L-Link? Huh? What IS that? Why did it go up again? What does it do?
after the suppression is over.
Swift, Chainlink and all their banking buddies need a narrative for when that happens.
The network is supposed to be kinda crypto-native and fair.
And the point is that all this new banking system is fair and most importantly, you had your chance to get in, goy, you just had to buy while the token was being fairly distributed! Anyone could've bought!

62269789
>>62269780
nobody cares about fairness though. vc tech companies ipo and dump on retail all the time, nobody cares

62269792
>>62269789
We are not talking about VC scams dumping on retail.
We are talking about Swift/DTCC/Euroclear et al building the future of finance.

62269820
>>62269792
but nobody cares about fairness for the unwashed masses. what happens in the real world:
>here is this new system
>oh ok, i guess i'll have to deal with that now, like all the other things that are out of my control
>i can buy something like sock in the system? ok cool, i also don't buy co2 certificates, but nice having the option i guess

62269824
>>62269820
You are not ready for the amount of seethe that is to come when LINK is around Google's marketcap.

62269828
>>62269824
can't be worse than the current linkie seethe, i'll manage

62269848
>>62269433
A spiritual war between good and evil.
Currently looking for an alternative to this situation.
Evil collapses through self sabotage on its own because it depends on light like a parasite.
The solution was to do nothing and ignore them.
But then why is it like this?
Are they really that stupid?

62269920
>>62263230
I dumped LINK already for BTC, I held on for years and got nothing but dump. Now I'm all in on BTC and I staked some now that native BTC staking is a thing and I might lend through TBV when its ready.

62270087
>>62268743
Exactly. This anon gets it. Link baggies are literal gme retards schizoing out about hedge fund "short ladder attacks" suppressing them. It seems that every bagholder tends to want to believe in the narrative that """they""" are messing with your bags.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 243 replies — 190.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 243 • Updated: 2026-05-24 22:26 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264927
>>62259968
Marry a rich bride, retard. Fucking low class roastoid marrying nigger, just kill yourself loser.

62264930
>>62259980
Where do all you currycels come from? Unbelievable.

62265011
>>62260025
Surrogacy. Be a single dad.

62265250
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s

62265283
>>62259968
>marry
>because one divorce
Then don't marry?
>muh keedz
You can have them without marrying
>muh common law
Move somewhere where that's not a thing, or recycle through women.
>>62259993
>monk
You can enjoy life without living paycheck to paycheck. Sex is basically free if you find the right girl. Packing homemade food and eating somewhere new every weekend costs less than fast food.

62265591
>>62260376
>who's going to pay for my kids now
He will through taxes

62265814
>goy wants to be free
uh oh, stinky

62265828
>>62260306
You just need to have some imagination to make it feel more fun but most men are retarded simp faggots and they need to rely on a woman's bullshit to feel good when they fuck. Women learn to turn it into a fun artificial challenge so it hypes you up.

62265831
>>62263540
>you know, actual
Normalfag subhuman brain rot. You're too retarded to enjoy your free time. You have nothing to teach to your spawn.

62265857
>>62263428
Normalfags can only think in binary terms. They're literal cattle and they turn suicidal without a master's guidance. When normalfags get rich they go insane and start coping with drugs and prostitutes until they die of STDs, OD or suicide. They can't handle the freedom.

62265866
>>62264003
Ok it's not the same, it's a matter of time.
If you've been working for a year and don't have 10k saved you're fucked. If you've been working for 6 years and don't have $100k you're also fucked. And yes $10k is more like a short-term emergency cushion against day-to-day issues where $100k is more like a longer term yearish cushion that may be enough to comfortably make small investments in one's business or other opportunities without threatening that cushion.

Basically the normie never saves that much. Rather than thinking "I need to get enough money together so my investments pay for everything I want forever without drawing down the balance" normies just think about making payments for next month.

62265872
>>62262791
The egregore of society doesn't even give a shit that you think it doesn't deserve it. It just imports desperate people from war torn poverty stricken shitholes instead.

62265874
>>62262844
>>62261211
How is offering the system your children to use as taxcattle a fuck you to it?

62265878
>>62260844
>debt free
Meme. I'm debtmaxxing harder than the US of Pissrael.

62265882
>>62261114
(You)r penis is too pure to allow it to be defiled by a wench's vaginal secretions.

62265887
>>62261238
>swingers
military fag brainrot leads to cockrot
>>62262206
they're trying to buck break us into flipping burgers and it's working

62265888
>>62259935
You know you can easily have biological children without marriage right?

Just fucking cum in some young woman and keep doing it until ahe misses a period. If that makes you fags squeamish just pay some surrogate to carry your child while you enjoy escorts or casual dating and sex.

It really isn't that hard, and after the surrogate pushes your child out it'll be the last time you need to hear a woman yell at you ever again lol.

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 92 replies — 67.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-05-24 22:21 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261340
>>62257392
>>62261260
Nothing wrong with being cautious, just don’t treat all BTCFi the same. Big names like a16zcrypto, Ledger, GoMining and others wouldn’t even touch it if everything was complete garbage.

62261342
>>62252488
50k? Nooo way

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

62262278
>>62259397
Buy altcoins too

62262820
>>62261608
No need to rush. People will figure it out eventually. You can already see more attention shifting toward DeFi outside the usual Ethereum-only lane anyway.

62262829
>>62261131
And people still wonder why institutions keep accumulating BTC.

62262834
>>62256924
Depends on their choice tho

62262852
Bitcoin is the future

62262893
>>62262278
>Hbar
>Link
>Rave
>Sei

62264806
>>62262852
And the present

62265221
>>62258793
Devil

62265362
Buy 10btc

62267557
>>62262893
Not bad

62267594
>>62250313
I'd be happy for $500 kek

62268019
I am aiming for having 0,1 BTC by the end of the year (got a nasty 3'000$ tax bill in august and september) but aside from that I'm going all in every month from now.

62268405
>>62260090
with what money

62268430
>>62253499
Checked. I appreciate the assessment.

62268528
>>62268430
When moon?

62269040
>>62268019
going all in is not recommended idiot

62269062
>>62269040
I can handle it.

62269119
>>62269040
if you have very little moeny, going all in is your only shot. You either make it or you stay poor a little longer, status quo.

62269122
>>62269119
*money

62269256
>>62269062
Then good luck

62269965
>>62246438
Less than 0.1 kek, but I'm buying more. I'm thinking of staking too, I noticed some BTCFI now gives a chance to stake without wrap or bridge.

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 26 replies — 35.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-24 22:59 UTC

OP

KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE

Early replies (first 3)

62269301
>>62269297
Oh thank God!

62269302
Won’t do shit
Trump won’t allow it

62269305
Can this nigga wait until btc dumps to $30k plsss

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269309
>Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's unconventional strategy aims to shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—while cutting short-term interest rates. By removing the Fed from the market, he intends to pop pandemic-era asset bubbles, lower broader mortgage rates, and return housing affordability to historical norms

62269311
20k October

62269319
>>62269309
Deporting 100 million would also solve housing.

62269321
>>62269297
imagine taking out a 100k 5x leveraged investment and it drops 20k in value while index funds go up like 40%

62269323
>>62269319
Anything but solving the real issue. Kick the can down the road enough and you'll go too far to turn back before you know it.

62269325
>>62269319
They've started going after financial institutions who service illegals and will probably start going after other institutions too in order to force illegals to self-deport. So it appears that they're doing both

62269418
>>62269297
So?

62269424
>>62269309
>KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE
Lowing rates increases the price of housing, trump with his real estate assets said he won't let pricing come down.

62269437
is it over?

62269442
>>62269319
Why not 200 million?

62269443
>>62269301
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ce-LjCpserA
Perfect time for a gif of this.

62269468
>>62269424
Trump is cooked after midterms. Lame duck

62269473
>>62269437
nah just keep buying

62269536
>>62269473
It that my nigga palmbeach? Haven't seen that nigga since the Boca bash in 2018.

62269553
>>62269468
More like a lame cuck desu

62269564
>>62269297
>this guy
>popping anything more than a pimple on his ass
pick 1 and only 1

62269581
>>62269442
because veggies won’t pick up themselves

62269586
>>62269309
>shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—
so, short the shit out of the stock market?

>cutting short-term interest rates.
wtf?

>>62269424
>>62269468
they really want to tank the value of the dollar lmao

>\(\$2\)
bot?

62269589
>>62269309
>pandemic-era asset bubbles
Is it finally time to short GME to zero?

62269600
>>62269319
Jews won't allow it

>>62269325
Retard

62270026
>>62269536
you know it is, playa!

62270033
Interest rates go down prices go up. Interest rates go up prices go up. What do you all not understand?

62270069
>>62270033
chekt, rekt and kekt

item.title: /Biz revival — 92 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-05-24 20:42 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266178
>>62265918
Id make fun of you for holding my nuts let be honest here.

62266203
>>62265467
hey man, I hope you get cancer

62266239
We’ve all moved to Twitter and telegram you fucking retard. Imagine still holding tech alts like link and faggot ass lte. If you’re in crypto and you don’t either 1) buy memecoins or 2) haven’t picked up perp trading bitcoin, you’re fucking hopeless. This place will never be what it used to be because of you FAGGOT JEET JANNIES KILL YOURSELVES. That’s the most you’ll get out of me. I’ll go back to never posting again because anyone left posting here is a braindead monkey.
>hurrdurrr my hype is up 50 percent
That’s cute. My troll and neet is up like a thousand percent.

62266242
>>62266203
Hah, no no it was me he was calling an aids infected faggot, I'm the anon who nearly died
>btw OP was at my doctors getting augmentin since I'm blowing huge clots out of my nose.
I don't mind him, least he's forcing engagement which is better than most people here.
At this point it's friendly banter.

62266392
In reality we need a revival of the human spirit, money is mostly pointless and only a currency used to exchange for goods and services, but in reality it's even less than that it's more akin to a warm fire or a security blanket.
What men are lacking is love for each other, not in the reddit gay sense either but as others said putting value in people.
Even OP who I was in sincere disagreement with has value, he pushed me to change my current circumstances by shifting my behavior.
I wasn't doing well the other day for a multitude of deep reasons, if OP even knew he wouldn't probably have had such harsh discourse with me but I don't even mind because every action has a reaction.

And I thought it was cute when I saw him actually worried if I had done something, he's on the road despite his anger, this place gambling/money changing in general tends to bring the worst out in us.
https://youtu.be/0x1StsL1zok

A revival of brotherhood is more important than the revival on making money, but even OP is craving that in some deeper intention, community is what you seek, but you do need to seek from within first to realize there's no value in this world if we can't see the potential in other people.

For example in the medical field I don't get much sincere engagement with doctors, I'm a complicated patient which scares doctors away from usually treating me with digity, and even worse usually treating me at all due to fear of being sued.
But one of my doctors who treats me like a human I saw today and gave me what I needed, because he knows the human side of medicine, he puts value in me because I bring him a unique side of medicine most doctors don't get to see (extremely rare syndromes) while also hearing about my personal life which he's amazed I'm able to do much at all considering how much I have on my plate, he met me after two major car accidents I was involved in before knowing I was a case study.

62266433
>>62265467
I respect you for trying to do something positive. /biz/ does suck ass lately.

In anycase, I'll ask a question. Why the hell is NEAR pumping that hard? Are people actually using it?

62266436
>>62265525
do NOT buy RBLX for more than a trade. accounting fraud

62266459
>>62266392
ok baldie, can you try to bring actual financial value instead of constantly whining like a bitch about your miserable existence?
We're not doing communism. Socialized healthcare is ok in fact i already have it so i don't think about it at all.

62266474
>>62266459
Where are you from, canada?
and no not /pol/ the fact you still think in isms is something.

62266478
>>62266459
Why do you have that saved.....

62266483
>>62266478
>62266478
zesty buckos are fun. And i can always post this when a brown person attacks the white race. I like your pepe, adding it to my meme folder

62266484
>>62266459
>>62266474
>>62266478
Third question are you the anime posting zoomer anon asking for tiktok links the other day?
Since there's only three of us here now.

62266493
>>62266483
If you're zesty buck posting should at the very least have the two where the twink buck is feeding the faggot who somehow looks like a lesbian woman.

62266502
>>62266484
no

from western europe btw. Not telling you were it's a faggy country
>>62266493
post it. do u post on /pol too? ibet you goon to hentai, never visited any other board than pol or biz

62266521
>>62266502
No I actually was the anon who got people to turn on tranime posting here lol, I never got into anime besides Berserk but that feels like gospel at this point everyone knows that.
I don't have it saved lol, I came from /pol/ after being a lolbert activist in the 2010s and stumbled my way almost to 100k in 4 months of being on /biz/ from being broke.
Then lost most of it, then met multiple millionaires, and then talked to Mark Cuban during the bullrun it was a wild experience.

I don't really post on /pol/ unless Israel is being bombed.

62266580
>>62266521
yes pol is too cringe and brown nowadays, i still go there to hurl racial insults from time to time and uplift my mood.
Biz is also very bad with 0 financial value but occasionnaly you make a valuable discovery. it's where i discovered LITS (yeah i know you don't like litecoin)
The reality is that it takes a lot of digging to find gems.

62266591
>>62266580
I'm just giving you shit, I've made money off litecoin multiple times.
and still the cheapest to transfer money around like for example you need to move 10k from coinbase to kraken, using BTC or ETH is gonna cost you an arm and a leg.
LTC is remarkably cheaper.

62266600
>>62266580
Last attempt I had was 10k on a coin that just released all Althea on Kraken, absolutely got buttfucked bought at 4 dollars when it released 6 dollars, looked like a solid project and was early............
...
Dropped to 4 cents think it's still there, but probably a good investment now, not when I bought.

62266609
An anon tries to create a decent thread and it gets instantly sabotaged.

62266618
>>62266600
>>62266591
that's why i buy litecoin. I don't want to buy something that will go to 0, i'm not looking for a x100 which is too hard to find.
Rn at 50$ ltc, has very little downside and a lot of upside. Even if it does a 3x at best, that's a relatively safe and ez 3x. Of course it could go lower even 0 everything is possible but highly unlikely.
And it could moon in the future because everyone uses it and somehow it hasn't shined yet

62266643
>>62265467
Akash, DePin actually seems to be getting some traction

62267090
>>62266618
it would have to get hacked or somehow so toxic everyone delists it even then only way its going to 0 is if btc goes to 0

62267267
>>62267090
i'll look into it but it's already 2x / 3x from bottom

62267325
>>62267267
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4vF3o4Ss6g

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

item.title: There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP — 44 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-05-24 22:05 UTC

OP

There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP

Early replies (first 3)

62267362
Im not poor I have a pressure washing business

62267365
>>62267358
decided to try again with the name covered this time?

the old thread is still up, we can see the name on the card

even if it gets taken down, it's in the archives

>The surname Kepnes is of Ashkenazi Jewish origin, with roots in Eastern European communities (such as Ukraine and Poland).

62267382
>>62267358
whats that one? need over 100k in account or something? what's it do?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267964
>>62267941
do you even understand how credit cards work you dumb retard? its interest free money as long as you pay it off in 30 days

62267992
>>62267964
yeah i'm asking if you're making over 100k, care about dumbass credit cards that grant you elite perks like airport lounge access (lol....lmao even), AND carry a balance (because you will, because you're a dumb faggot who cares about shit like this), are you still paying a 25% interest rate?

62268001
>>62267992
generally no. After the 12 month introductory period the interest rate for a millionaire is in the range of about 6%

this is bumped up a bit by the annual fee.

62268003
>>62268001
thank you. that is an actual perk.

62268004
>>62268001
I'm still shocked there are retards out there who pay an annual fee for credit cards. Unbelievable.

62268005
>>62267992
it also depends on how much you spend

they charge merchants about 3% for a processing fee, so the interest rates to a consumer can be zero or even negative if you spend enough money. They'll make their interest off the vendor. And rich people can buy tens or even hundreds of thousands on credit for free while the card company still makes bank.

technically you're still paying the merchant's share of 3%, but nominally you're paying zero.

62268007
>>62268003
6% is high
I can borrow against my house right now for less than 3% so cards aren't super attractive unless you're writing off the interest

which most rich people do.

62268008
>>62268005
yeah different hustle same game just to drive spend on the card makes sense.

62268010
>>62268004
>I'm still shocked there are retards out there who pay an annual fee for credit cards. Unbelievable.
it's an entirely different calculus when you're
1. writing off the fees on your taxes, probably as a business expense
2. getting perks and upgrades in exchange for the fee

rich people math isn't the same as normal budgeting. taxes become a lot more important the higher you go

62268015
>>62268010
an example

say I'm a millionaire business owner taxed at 50% (35% plus 15% SE tax ignoring the overlap cutoff).

If I pocket $100 I owe $50 to the IRS

If I waste $100 on a product and get $75 worth of perks in exchange, I came out ahead. Because I lost $100 but gained $75 tax-free, which is better than only having $50 after tax

this is the strategy behind lots of business expenses. I'm technically throwing money away, but I get more in return than if I had kept the money and paid taxes on it.

62268020
>>62268015
this is also the basis of most B2B sales

If you pay me $100 for a product and I deliver $75 worth of product I made $25 and you still came out ahead because after taxes you would have only kept $50 anyways.

businesses are fine with spending money on stuff people would consider pointless because it's cheaper than paying taxes

62268022
>>62267358
I have the BMO World Elite Mastercard with 5% cashback.

Questrade will also be launching a couple of credit card products this fall, one of them will be the Questrade Reserve Card for their most elite members, which will have 6% cashback.

62268044
>>62268022
why do the rich just get richer in our system?
what's with the invisible poor tax on everyone ontop of normal taxes?

62268049
>>62268044
rich people find scalable free money hacks while wagies are on a fixed income that can't be increased beyond the number of hours they can work.

the reason the poor pay so much more for everything is simple. That extra poor tax is exactly where the rich people get their money

62268055
>>62267834
I have this card, it’s not a flex. 150k cad is hobo tier in this country, also nobody will every recognize the card so hardly a status symbol even if you consider it one

62268064
>>62268055
is the cutoff in CAD?

I bought a timeshare once that had a cutoff of $70k USD to apply. The cutoff in CAD was higher. Whatever the exchange rate was.

62268072
>>62268064
Yes, Wealthsimple is for Canadians only. We have the most kiked banks so this platform is good by comparison. Still can’t do stuff like mortgages, business loans, big purchases or withdraw for physical cash though so still need a regular bank to off ramp to if you want to use your money for these things.

62268085
>>62268072
interesting. You guys are even more fucked on real estate than we are from what I've seen here

62268623
>>62268022
>5% on groceries capped at $500 spending per month then it goes to 1%
KEK

62268692
Is this a marketing thread? Should I get this? I just think it would be tedious to transfer over money to my ws from my main bank to use it unless I can have it autopay from the other account which I'm sure I can? I'm pulling around 250k so I can get the fancy version too. I feel like I never use any of those perks, maybe the 2% back would be ok? I just have a basic bitch scotia momentum right now.

62268807
>>62268085
300k CAD (220k USD) for average 1 BDRM condo in boring smallish town (50-100k people) and 1 million CAD (~720k USD) for basic detached family home in suburbia with small lawn

If you go to middle of nowhere I am talking towns of like 1k people up north where weather is horrible, prices go down to ~400k for a basic house and 150k for a condo

We also have nightmare food prices and nightmare taxes. Canada is a peak humiliation ritual for younger people

62268875
>>62267358
>>62267305
>>62268155

>>62268692
>Is this a marketing thread? Should I get this?
It has to be, there is no way someone (whose last name is Kepnes, probably) isn't getting paid to post this shit twice.
Who the fuck thinks 4chan is a good place for this, idk.

62268897
>>62267666
150k canadian is nothing in real money, that's like a low London salary. Why would you flex that?

62269164
>>62268875
if it was, i'd be shilling hard the perks. I'm just dabbing on all the leafs who don't have a VIP on this board.

62269230
>>62267362

Based blue collar business owner. Make sure to take your Mexican employees out for tacos once a month

62269308
>>62267358
>Measuring how much money you have by the possession of a financial instrument whose sole pupose is spending money
poor detected

62269358
>>62269308
cope more poorfag

62269398
>>62268010
>>62268015
> If I waste $100 on a product and get $75 worth of perks in exchange, I came out ahead. Because I lost $100 but gained $75 tax-free, which is better than only having $50 after tax

This is literally the equivalent of clipping coupons not "rich people math" or whatever the fuck you said.

62269595
>>62268044
You need a good CPA to work all of the tax benefits and write-offs on your behalf.

If you run a small corporation (ie. an incorporated day trading business that earns more than $200k per year), you can roll a shit ton of that money into an IPP that you can write off against your business income. Once you're over 40, the contribution limit starts going up a lot.

Create a flippable small side business and you get up to $1.25 million in lifetime capital gains exemption when you go to sell it.

Tax planning and strategizing, even for individuals with just an RRSP and TFSA, over several years can make the difference in having a few extra hundred thousand or even millions in your investment accounts that you can then compound. For example, saving up RRSP contribution room in your 20s and early 30s until you're making six figures, then slamming 50% or more of your income into it each year to drop your effective tax rate down significantly.

There's the RRSP gross up strategy, using $10-20k credit line as a bridge loan to contribute in February, and using your tax refund to pay it off in April/May.

Most Canadians can't think or plan beyond the next two weeks.

62269916
>>62268897
cuckada is a poorfag country. 150k personal income puts you into the 90th percentile

item.title: Final Chance to change your life Forever — 32 replies — 23.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Final Chance to change your life Forever

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-24 22:19 UTC

OP

Based on all my analysis, the stock is going to PUMP HARD by early July to August. Final chance to get rich instantly with 100% return in a few months or 10x returns in a few years.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dm_BrGu1sHM

You can chase your fantasy. It's not a mirage.

Early replies (first 3)

62268810
Value trap kek. Tencent will take it private.

62268838
>>62268804
Is Ubi still DEI and Sweet Baby approved?

62268842
>>62268810
What does that mean for shareholders? Won’t they have to buy my shares or some shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62268847
>>62268804
Honestly I’m a bit intrigued here. The company has valuable IP and I could easily see a resurgence.

62268871
>>62268847
>The company has valuable IP
It's already been partially and is getting progressively carved out by Tencent. Dogshit company needs to pay them to use their original IPs.

62268907
>>62268804
A remake won’t save them, they need like to get rid of all DEI staff and replace with nerdiest white and Asian autists if they want their company to survive

62268922
>>62268804
They deserve their downfall if they're remaking a game from the ps4 era and trying to bait people into paying 100 dollars for a 5% quality increase by making the cover art some polished globohomo turd art.

62268923
>samefag shill posts this same retarded shit over and over

62268942
>>62268810
Tencent was trying to buy out Ubisoft a few years back but a major sticking point was that Tencent wanted to clean house of Ubisoft's leadership but they refused to step down. The entire reason the company has been in shambles is because of their horrendous leadership.

62268949
>>62268804
I see them folding as the most likely option

62268983
>>62268838
Yes, black flag remake is already confirmed as very censored.

62269009
that marketcap is insane when minecraft was sold for 2.5 billion a decade ago, its easily worth a billion.

62269032
>>62269009
their enterprise value is around $3bn, so it's still overvalued considering their current inability to service future debt payments even with the stock dilution. However, looking purely at the chart, it looks like OP is right and something definitely is brewing.

62269042
ha a fellow ubichad.

I don't get it tho my shares are worth 5.38 bought the dip at 4.
What's the difference with regular ubi shares

62269047
>>62269009
Historically we’ve already seen UBI soaring 10x or even 20x after a downturn, several times.

The problem is that UBI can remain a value trap for many years now. I need more evidence that they’re cooking something convincing.

62269069
>>62268804
>negative pe ratio
>in debt
>flagship IPs already partially owned in a different holding company
>woke durring the woke-fatigue part of the cycle
I know they started restrucutering things, but shit seems pretty cooked bro. I do however regret not buying on the blackflag rumors/leaks and selling the pump that came from the official announcement

62269080
>>62269069
it's not cooked, they have a bunch of assets and streaming rights that should price the stock around 15 euros (currently it's 5). If they manage to get their shit together and some good games come out they're going to 10x. (which is not guaranteed but likely considering they're losing a lot of money so they have to change their approach.)

62269255
UP ubichads, raise up. For once a good pick

>>62269047
am i retarded if i don't care to wait 3 years or so for a 10x?

62269266
Someone post the jeet staff picture

62269273
>>62268922
This. If they have to reboot then atleast do the entire Ezio Collection.

62269306
>>62268983
DEI and Sweet Fagbaby have nothing to do with censorship unless you mean censoring out straight white guy characters and adding in shitskins and freakshows.

62269510
saaaar you must buy the bags of the woke jeetslop saaaar

62269713
>>62269510
yes, this is the new biz stock
>he is literally not holding it
nvgmi

62269716
>>62269306
They also censor cleavage and other "problematic" details tailored to the male gaze.

62269721
How pozzed are their upcoming releases?

62269752
They should make good games again, not some nigger samurai goyslop.

62269757
if gta flops (which it will) this will follow, then the rest of the gaming industry will die, video games are dying

62269758
somehow this piece of shit still feels overvalued

62269782
>>62268847
Name three companies that pulled off a resurgence ever since becoming woke AND poorly made. Call of duty is resurging. Halo isn't resurging. Fallout isn't resurging.

62269827
massive /v/ fag here who follows the industry.

Heres the thing with this.

The owners/family of Ubisoft are flat out fucking retarded and French. Internally they are oversaturated with toxic positivity and literal faux progressiveness but BELIEVE IT fully(this is important) its past DEI.

Yes they have IPs but dev cycles are long, 4-6-8 years. Games like theirs cost shitloads of money(they dont actually but they spend it) and generally run tandem Dev teams to hit cycles with proper pacing for the audience. Example(this is just fake)

Ass Creed starts pre-production----> 1 year ----> Far Cry starts Pre-Production----> 2 years ----> Ass creed in production ---->3 years ----> Far Cry production ----2 years but its total 3 still.

Ass Creed Marketing into final release-----> 4th year-----> release ----Far Cry in 3rd Year-----> 5th year Far Cry releases 4 years with marketing leading.

You have to take in consideration WHO is leading them, whos on the teams, and how retarded they are being, Ubisoft has a fantastic track record of being retarded AND not adjusting the course.

Nintendo and Capcom DO act retarded, then adjust course, especially Capcom, basically their MO. Ubisoft has been on a chronic implosion with no end in sight.

Investing in this is a gamble, it can pay off, but this is the final shot. Might as well since stocks are so cheap but dont expect it to go anywhere with a gureentee. They are backed by French Tax money from the government as well. They were called out for being retarded in one of the last shareholder meetings specifically about DEI shit. They doubled down.

If they sell of IPs Ubisoft doesnt gain value, they lose it. The fact they are on a trove of IPs is solid, but if they keep fucking it up, their image will just finally go past the point of no return. They have a dogshit and invasive PC platform forced on you to play their shit games EVEN if bought through steam.

The games are worse, support is worse, overall its bad.

62269947
>>62269827
Frenchfag here, the owners of Ubisoft are from Brittany, which is the HQ of woke leftist dreamers completely disconnected from reality. Employees (who mostly look like leftards when you see them protesting) also say that the CEO is senile like Trump. So yeah, not a good look.

>>62269255
UBI spends so much it could also go under and be bought by Tencent for €1 in the end. That’s the risk.

62269958
>>62268842
Yes

>>62265215

62268848
>>62268839

62268854
>>62268848
I have no felonies anon

62268856
>>62268848
>>62268854
Oh I see you're referring to my ID. Yeah, sometimes I get an odd one.

62269819
>>62265215
>humiliation ritual
ESLs really are so retarded. You don't know what that means

62269825
>>62269819
I'm EOP not ESL, and I was using le meme phrase

62269861
>>62265215
ETH fanboy following in his master's footsteps. Pathetic!

62269866
>>62269861
I have 0 (zero) ETH. I've never had it.

62269892
>>62265137
I sold and bought BTC, staked it, see you 2030.

62269902
>>62268843
>>62268562
>>62268252
it's obviously not vitalik you retard.

item.title: retarded baggies and newfags were calling for a supercycle — 26 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
retarded baggies and newfags were calling for a supercycle

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-24 21:11 UTC

OP

LMAO

Early replies (first 3)

62265421
I will be buying at 40k during bottomtober along with all of the smart hairdressers and Uber drivers!

62265427
>>62265421
me too, unironically

62265429
>>62265421
hairdressers and uber drivers were spamming the super cycle meme. OGs know that Bitcoin ALWAYS follows the 4 year cycle

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265446
>>62265367
Every cycle the same retards call the same thing about supercycle and unironically they will be the ones capitulating once BTC reaches the bottom. Lmfao they literally fell for the CZ and jewish bait and they are the ones that create this long squeeze. I hope there are more of them so I can keep printing with my shorts.

62265621
>>62265367
The supercycle is in my pants!

62267493
>>62265621
post them.

62267515
>>62265429
Bitcoin doesn't follow anything.
Retail traders follow the 4 year cycle. Institutions do not, and they outweigh retail sell pressure by a lot.

62267841
>>62265367
oh no we get to DCA for a few more years.
How is this a bad thing if you're even correct?
This would be amazing if we get to DCA low prices for months or years.

62267849
If these baggies built conviction they're sitting on nice gains even if 40k comes to fruition.

62267875
>>62267515
See you in October, baggie

62267892
>>62267515
Institutions hold only 10% of BTC’s market cap (or up to 20% if you count ETFs, but these are mostly retail in disguise). And among them, 95% of all institutional buying is done by just one company: MSTR. And it’s basically just one person taking the key decisions in the end.

Not a very solid barrier against the impeding turbulence where retail represents 90% of all buying and selling.

62268958
>>62265367
I sold waiting for the October meme. Couple hundred k's ready to launch

62268967
>>62265421
All of them are out though. The "mainstream" calling for october bottom are still 0.01% of the population. No-one in the world is looking at crypto right now.

62269011
>>62268967
And the majority looking are retards that think the bottom isn't in and beliee we are still in a bullrun. Just check X and tradingview's community analysis.

Also if you tell normies the bottom isn't in and BTC can drop 30%+ in a single month, they will laugh at your face, even though there's a chart proving that several times

62269020
>>62267892
I'm not arguing against that. However, the ratio of institutions to retail will be very different when CLARITY is signed.

62269092
>>62269020
Feel free to correct me, but seen from abroad, this "Clarity Act" thing looks like a bunch of regulatory details from one single country, wrapped in bullshit that won’t change anything, at least in the short term. The framework alone is not enough to create significant institutional demand for crypto in general and BTC in particular. Any institution, anywhere in the world, that wants to buy BTC can already do it; no one has been waiting for American regulations. Yet the worldwide demand remains very tame.

62269242
>>62265367
Oh good thing I sold and bought up a little more stock to diversify my portfolio. I was prepared for it to go either way with new stock and the old stock I am still holding onto are things that can survive this. I guess I will buy cheepies to lower my average for the next 2 years.

62269249
>>62269242
Oh wait this is a buttcoin thread.

62269293
>>62265367
This is what I believe is going to happen. Thoughts?

62269299
>>62269293
$140k- 150k top in 2029.

62269304
>>62269299
I was thinking that in 2029 with a 3.5x growth rate after looking at diminishing returns. I was going for optimistic but will definitely DCA offload from 150K

62269361
>>62265367

62269367
>>62269092
The idea is that CLARITY allows US corporations to transact with cryptocurrencies and use crypto-integrated networks for data computation and storage.
Once this is proven successful, other nations with tight crypto regulations will follow.

62269370
>>62269293
19,9k>68,9>126,2>182k

62269779
>>62265367
>>62265429
there is no cycle, Dr. Pseud
it's a random walk

62269809
>>62265367
crypto kiddies are fucking retarded, they got addicted to their bitfuck tokens in 2017 and 2020 and have been holding out for a repeat of massive gains in the alt space since then.

All they had to do was research a little more and diversify into tech/AI/energy/mining stocks and they would be making millions today with the six figures they managed to gross in the 2017-2020 runs that they have since completely given back to the markets.

Fucking retards.

item.title: Day 9 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 16 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 9 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-24 21:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62268550
thats impossible on every imaginable levels!

62268555
>>62268550
that’s what they said about Apple

62268559
>>62268555
even if i dont apple, they do produce phones, tables, laptops and so on. iseecrap does nothing!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62268564
>>62268559
ICP is the Amazon web service alternative, the only alternative coin in the sphere that is fully on chain with chain fusion. Have you even seen Caffeine.ai?

So when you say nothing that’s just fucking ignorant.

62268567
>>62268564
>Caffeine.ai?

what has this site to do with icp?
they ask for hard $$$

62268597
>>62268567
Everything you make on Caffeine is stored on ICP web services.

Also the government of Pakistan is building on ICP.

62268802
>>62268567
Look up Hbar and ICP partnership

62268888
Please fuck my ass

62268917
>>62268597
Pajeet detect, gtfo scum of the world

62268941
>>62268917
Who hurt you saar?

62269272
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62269292
>>62269272

you are a bot and you always say the same thing.

62269298
>>62269292
You missed a day or 2

62269749
>>62269298
I was banned

62269756
>>62269749
can we have that again!!!

62269865
>>62269749

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 152 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 152 • Updated: 2026-05-24 00:33 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264946
>>62264122
Based

62264980
>>62262862
That's common here in New Zealand as well.
I've seen people turn up with;

A whole cooked chicken and nothing else.
A 12 pack of bread rolls and a tin of Tuna.
One large bag of carrots.
A tin of Baked Beans and no can opener.
A loaf of bread and a smoked chicken breast.
Half a dozen cooked snags.
Plain rice and a chicken drumstick.
Two Sea Urchins.
A cheesy loaf.
A banana bunch.

Last nights dinner is very common.

62265020
>>62262598
you can put a loaf of bread in the fridge to make it last longer, anon

62265030
>>62263602

62265033
>>62263530
they are too retarded to understand that the shiny rocks are what go into their computers to design and make their pokemon cards

62265037
>>62265020
depends on conditions, more important for bread is air tightness and moister.. putting it in some fridges can make it go faster depending.. you can freeze bread and defrost it as you need pretty fast though

62265043
>>62265037
For toast putting the bread in the freezer is just sensible.

62265138
>>62265020
Just stick it in a bread bin like a normal person

62265148
>>62262449
boomer here

I was 30 years old when I could afford to eat at a restaurant for anything other than a special occasion. I was 40 when I could afford to eat at a restaurant whenever I wanted.

younger generations don't get a lot of pity for demanding luxuries we worked our asses off for and they think are just rights every person deserves.

same with houses. And new cars. Earn it. It took us decades of work.

62265153
>>62265138
Look at this fancy fuck with his bread humidor.

62265271
I'm bringing bread and cheese and ham and some fucking canned soup with me to Stockholm next week to beat the Swedish jew and there's nothing you can do to stop me. And some booze in my checked bag. Buying alcohol in Sweden will bankrupt a millionaire.

62265280
>>62262630
To be honest you can make bread on the griddle, so the bread maker is just wasted money.

62265288
>>62265271
Buy Felix' Gulasch when you're there. It is a yummy soup.

62265303
>he doesn’t make his own bread

62265600
>>62265288
We have soup here where I live. I don't need to pay 10€ for a can or jar of Swedish goulash slop.

62265622
>check thread
>It's still an Indian psyop to get you to eat bread for every meal and live like a prisoner and be happy
I hate this board now

62265722
Isn't it obvious this entire thing is just the uber business model being deployed to food?

1. Establish yourself in an area
2. Undercut the competition
3. Make as many people as possible rely on your service
4. Establish a near monopoly
5. Raise prices
6. Profit

I mean people like to point out that the fast food prices went up during covid and blamed the money printing and rising wages. That neglects the people who were serious about the lockdown basically not leaving the house and just ordering delivery over and over until they got used to the idea. Then loan shark services like klarna drops by and offers people a way to extend the use even when you don't have money.

I mean one of the delivery companies literally is Uber.

62266666
>>62262474
Shartistanis pay $5 for a loaf of bread and $12 for 18 bags of crisps? I just bought two loaves for 26p each (maybe 80c total) and isn't glyphosate filled foam rubber

62266673
>>62266666
CHECKED

62266688
>>62262474
he's right, that like $6500 for the year. spent on lunch.

i actually had this conversation with a guy i work with, when he was slinging weed he was going out to eat every night with his girlfriend, he realized he had been spending a fucking fortune on food. i've made every meal i've eaten for the last 4 months.

62266712
>>62262506
it's not going to get better cupcake. we are sprinting head-on into an existential crisis. either AI delivers utopia forever, or it delivers utopia for a year or two then wipes us out. no one knows because no one has time to make sure it's not acting sussy.

62266992
>>62263206
I spend less than 15 dollars a month on my gym membership and the cute mullato at the desk always says hi to me.

62267454
>>62265138
i don't think these do shit, they're not airtight, all they do is stop light from reaching the bread i guess.
i keep mine in the fridge with the bag cinched super tight, lasts for weeks, have no problems.

62267474
>>62262587
This is bullshit
No i will not be explaining

62267477
>>62262773
Is this bmw keys downie from ck?

62267510
>>62262587
bitch it takes 20 minutes to make spaghetti and half of that is sitting down while the water boils.
also, instead of being retarded, why not get a stool or something for your kitchen. shit, get a comfy office chair for all i care.

62267556
>>62264583
>how a retard boomer like Dave becomes worth hundreds of millions of dollars
The same way all of them did. By claiming greater knowledge and understanding and then selling it to you.
I have a six figure net worth and virtually no debt but I can manage my debt and understand the purpose of debt and I understand the economic problems he pretends don't exist and don't crow about people not wanting to literally eat shit for the rest of their lives in hopes that maybe one day they can buy a house that they'll never be able to afford because the same compound interest curve that grows your investments exponentially prices them out of the market exponentially. I'm not high on my own supply and recognize that prices have gone up far faster than wages, so it's not about "You're wasting money going out to eat". Like dude said, even if you're spending $28 for lunch every day, that's still only 10k a year that could be going towards your housing downpayment, but housing downpayments are no longer 20-30k, they're 100-200k.

62267568
>>62263206
What you and a lot of these other faggots don't seem to comprehend is that these subscriptions are part of the social dynamic today.
Yeah, you can stop spending money on netflix or your phone or your gym membership or whatever else you're doing other than eating shit and saving money for two decades for a downpayment, you're missing out on socializing with your community, you're missing out on being able to ask a girl back to netflix and chill, all things that are more important than saving up 50k for a downpayment on trailer that 25 years ago cost $10k. You hear me? A GODDAMNED TRAILER costs six figures today.
These people are not only detached from reality, they're all playing their part in the jewish-libertarian atomization of white society.

62267583
>>62262474
Are Gen Z even aware of that spare tire underneath their trunks?

62267662
>BEST ECONOMY EVER!!! THE DOW IS SO HIGH!!!! HOW DOES GOD EMPEROR TRUMP DO IT!?!??!
>...
>Uh.... guys can you stop whining about the cost of living...? Can't you just eat sawdust mixed with corn syrup or something? Your ruining the MAGA vibes, y'know?
